title: Why Italians Saw the Pope Very Differently - Ada Palmer
summary: A podcast episode featuring historian Ada Palmer discussing how Italians perceived papal authority and religious authority differently from other European populations. The episode examines historical perspectives on the Pope and Italian cultural attitudes toward religious institutions.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer joins Dwarkesh Patel to discuss how Italians perceived papal and religious authority differently from other European populations. The conversation examines historical perspectives on the Pope alongside Italian cultural attitudes toward religious institutions. The takeaway: proximity and local context shaped a distinct Italian view of an authority that the rest of Europe regarded through a different lens.
